title: Student Records
description: Student records in Conroe ISD are protected under clear guidelines about who can access them, how they are maintained, and how long they are kept. Parents and eligible students have the right to review educational records, request corrections, and control who outside the school can see their personal information. Understanding these rules helps families feel confident that their children's academic and personal information is handled with care and respect for their privacy.

timeline_local.summary: This update to Conroe ISD's Board Policy FL (Local) on Student Records adds a new item to the list of types of education records, specifically including records relating to school library materials that a student obtains from a school library.
changes_added: Records relating to school library materials the student obtains from a school library. (added as item 16 under Types of Education Records)
timeline_local.notes: The redline text for item 16 appears underlined in the document ("Records relating to school library materials the student obtains from a school library."), indicating it is newly added language. However, the document is a scanned PDF and color rendering is ambiguous — the underline formatting on item 16 was used to identify this as an addition rather than confirmed red color. No strikethrough/removed text was identified. The cover page title "Discuss and Possibly Adopt Revisions to Board Policy FL (Local)" maps to action = 'adopted' per the provided rules.

timeline_local.summary: This update to Conroe ISD's Student Records policy (FL Local) adds school library checkout records as a new category of education records maintained by the District. The addition explicitly includes "Records relating to school library materials the student obtains from a school library" in the enumerated list of education record types.
changes_added: Records relating to school library materials the student obtains from a school library. (added as item 16 in the Types of Education Records list)
timeline_local.notes: The redline is clear: item 16 in the Types of Education Records list is rendered in red underlined text, indicating it is a new addition. All other policy text appears in standard black (unchanged). The cover page title reads "Discuss Revisions to Board Policy FL (Local)," which maps to a first_reading action. No red strikethrough text was identified anywhere in the document.

description_local: This policy governs how the school district collects, maintains, and manages student records, including what types of documents count as official education records. It applies to all students enrolled in the district, as well as their parents or guardians who have rights related to accessing and protecting those records. A recent update now specifically includes library records as an official type of education record, which matters to families because it clarifies that information about a student's library activity is formally protected under the district's student privacy rules.
